steps.title: Factor to read off the roots
steps.content:
Shortcut (Vieta's): Factor out $(x - k)$:
$(x-k)^2 - (k-4a)(x-k) = (x-k)\bigl[(x-k)-(k-4a)\bigr] = (x-k)(x-2k+4a) = 0$
The two roots are $x = k$ and $x = 2k - 4a$.

steps.title: Use sum of roots = 3k + 35
steps.content:
Sum of roots: $k + (2k - 4a) = 3k - 4a$. Set equal to the given sum $3k + 35$:
$3k - 4a = 3k + 35 \Rightarrow -4a = 35 \Rightarrow a = -\dfrac{35}{4}$
Equivalent decimal: $-8.75$. The answer is -35/4.
